title: sambucus ebulus var. humilis
intro: Sambucus ebulus var. humilis, also known as dwarf elder or low elder, is a lesser-known variety of the elder plant, a shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Sambucus ebulus var. humilis lie in its berries, flowers, and roots, which contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function, making it a popular natural remedy for colds, flu, and other infections. Sambucus ebulus var. humilis can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion using the flowers, leaves, or berries, although it's essential to note that the berries should be used in moderation due to their toxicity.
